metadata.title: Why doubling your Claude Code session time doesn't double your output
metadata.purpose: Each session carries a context-accumulation cost that pays off early, then decays; past ~25–35 minutes, added tokens yield diminishing returns as working context fills and momentum fades.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.visual_object: A bell curve — PRs completed (y) vs. session duration (x) — peaking at 25–35 min, falling steeply past 60.
metadata.hook: There's an optimal session window — and a cliff past it where longer sessions ship fewer PRs.

beats.narration_text: A team averaging 40-minute sessions ships fewer PRs per sprint than one averaging 28 minutes, despite sessions 40% longer.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If Claude Code's value scaled with time invested, why does a 90-minute session sometimes merge fewer PRs than two focused 30-minute ones?
There's an optimal session window — and a cliff past it where longer sessions ship fewer PRs.
Each session carries a context-accumulation cost that pays off early, then decays; past ~25–35 minutes, added tokens yield diminishing returns as working context fills and momentum fades.
beats.visual: A bell curve — PRs completed (y) vs. session duration (x) — peaking at 25–35 min, falling steeply past 60.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Q1: 40-min avg sessions → 18 PRs/sprint. Q2: 28-min avg sessions, same usage → 26 PRs/sprint.
Each session carries a context-accumulation cost that pays off early, then decays; past ~25–35 minutes, added tokens yield diminishing returns as working context fills and momentum fades.
